topic.name: Alligation or Mixture
topic.description: Mixing quantities at different values using alligation and weighted averages. Replacement, adulteration, and ratio problems. Estimated reading 22 min; practice 38 min.

theory.introduction: Alligation is a rapid method to find the ratio in which two or more ingredients at different prices or concentrations must be mixed to obtain a mixture at a given mean value. Mixture problems also cover replacement, successive dilution, and finding pure quantity after adulteration.
theory.explanation:
CONCEPT EXPLANATION

Rule of Alligation: If two ingredients at prices (or values) A and B are mixed to give a mean price M, then
Quantity of cheaper / Quantity of dearer = (B − M) / (M − A).

The mean price of a mixture = (C1×Q1 + C2×Q2 + …) / (Q1 + Q2 + …). For replacement problems: after replacing x litres from a vessel of capacity V containing pure liquid with water, and repeating n times, pure quantity = V × (1 − x/V)^n.

REAL-LIFE APPLICATIONS

Blending alloys, mixing milk and water, preparing solutions in chemistry labs, and pricing blended products use alligation and mixture logic.

COMMON MISTAKES

1) Swapping the alligation ratio (putting M−A on the cheaper side incorrectly).
2) Forgetting to add quantities when finding total mixture amount.
3) Using replacement formula without raising to the power n for repeated operations.
4) Mixing price per kg with price per litre without consistent units.
5) Assuming equal quantities when only the ratio is asked.

IMPORTANT NOTES

• Cheaper ingredient connects to (dearer price − mean); dearer connects to (mean − cheaper price).
• Mean must lie between the two extreme values for a valid mix.
• In replacement, only the replaced fraction leaves each time.
• For three ingredients, extend weighted average or apply alligation stepwise.
• Percentage concentration = (quantity of solute / total quantity) × 100.

formulas.title: Rule of alligation (two ingredients)
formulas.content: Ratio (cheaper : dearer) = (dearer price − mean price) : (mean price − cheaper price)

formulas.title: Single replacement
formulas.content: After replacing x from V litres of pure liquid once with water, pure left = V(1 − x/V)

shortcutTips.title: Cross difference
shortcutTips.tip: Draw the alligation cross: differences from mean go on opposite sides of the ingredient.

shortcutTips.title: Mean between extremes
shortcutTips.tip: If mean is not strictly between the two prices, the mixture is impossible with those ingredients alone.

shortcutTips.title: Ratio not total
shortcutTips.tip: Alligation gives ratio; multiply by a common factor only when total quantity is specified.

shortcutTips.title: Replacement power
shortcutTips.tip: Each replacement multiplies the pure fraction by (1 − x/V); use exponent n for n replacements.

shortcutTips.title: Milk and water
shortcutTips.tip: For cost-based milk adulteration, treat water as Rs. 0 per litre when using alligation on prices.

shortcutTips.title: Reverse from mean
shortcutTips.tip: Given final concentration and one component, set up (pure/total) = concentration to find unknown quantity.

solvedExamples.problem: In what ratio must rice at Rs. 9 per kg be mixed with rice at Rs. 7 per kg so that the mixture is worth Rs. 8 per kg?
A 60-litre vessel contains pure milk. 12 litres are drawn off and replaced with water. This is done twice. How much milk remains?
steps: Each replacement removes 12/60 = 1/5 of current milk.

solvedExamples.problem: How many kg of sugar costing Rs. 8 per kg must be mixed with 15 kg of sugar costing Rs. 12 per kg so that the mixture costs Rs. 10 per kg?
solvedExamples.solution: 15 kg of the cheaper sugar is required.

solvedExamples.problem: A merchant has two types of tea worth Rs. 60/kg and Rs. 90/kg. He mixes them to sell at Rs. 75/kg. Find the mixing ratio.

exercise.title: General Questions
exercise.description: MCQ practice on alligation rule, mean price, mixture ratios, and replacement problems.

questions.question: In what ratio must wheat at Rs. 4.50 per kg be mixed with wheat at Rs. 5.25 per kg so that the mixture costs Rs. 4.95 per kg?
Tea at Rs. 60/kg is mixed with tea at Rs. 90/kg to get a mixture worth Rs. 75/kg. What is the ratio of cheaper to dearer tea?
A 40-litre container has pure milk. 8 litres are removed and replaced with water. How much milk remains?
Sugar at Rs. 8/kg and Rs. 12/kg are mixed in ratio 1:1. What is the mean price per kg?
In what ratio must a 20% alcohol solution be mixed with a 50% alcohol solution to get a 35% solution?
A vessel contains 50 litres of pure milk. 10 litres are drawn and replaced with water. How much milk is left?
Rice at Rs. 24/kg and Rs. 15/kg are mixed to obtain rice worth Rs. 18/kg. Find the ratio in which they must be mixed.
How many litres of a 30% acid solution must be mixed with 20 litres of a 60% acid solution to get a 45% solution?
A 80-litre cask contains pure wine. 16 litres are removed and replaced with water. This operation is repeated once. How much wine remains?
Two varieties of oil costing Rs. 50/litre and Rs. 70/litre are mixed in ratio 3:2. What is the cost of the mixture per litre?
A milkman mixes water (cost Rs. 0) with milk costing Rs. 40/litre to sell at Rs. 32/litre. In what ratio are milk and water mixed?
30 kg of an alloy costing Rs. 100/kg is mixed with 20 kg of another alloy costing Rs. 160/kg. Find the mean cost per kg.
A container has 100 litres of pure alcohol. 25 litres are replaced with water. What percentage of the mixture is alcohol?
In what ratio must Rs. 5 coins and Rs. 10 coins be mixed so that the average value is Rs. 7.50?
A 60-litre vessel of milk has 12 litres removed and replaced with water, done 3 times. How much milk remains?
How much water must be added to 30 litres of milk costing Rs. 4.80/litre so that the mixture is worth Rs. 3.60/litre?
A mixture of 45 litres contains milk and water in ratio 5:4. How much water must be added to make the ratio 5:6?
questions.explanation: Milk = 25 L, water = 20 L. Need 25/w = 5/6 ⇒ w = 30. Add 10 L water.

questions.question: Spirit at Rs. 80/litre is mixed with water to sell at Rs. 64/litre. What is the ratio of spirit to water?
A shopkeeper mixes two pulses in ratio 2:3 costing Rs. 40/kg and Rs. 55/kg. What is the selling price per kg if he wants 20% profit on cost?
A 25% salt solution and 75% salt solution are mixed in ratio 2:1. What is the concentration of the mixture?
From a 64-litre cask of wine, 8 litres are drawn and replaced with water. After 2 such operations, what fraction of the cask is wine?
Gold of 18 carat is mixed with gold of 24 carat to obtain 20 carat gold. Find the required ratio.
A chemist mixes 10 litres of 15% solution with x litres of 45% solution to get a 30% solution. Find x.
